sections.paragraph:
prop. to rub (compare the kindred מָרַת, and in Greek ἀμέργω, ὀμοργνύω); hence-

(1) to polish (metal); 2Ch 4:16, נְחשֶׁת מָרוּק “polished brass;” Jerem. 46:4 , מִרְקוּ חָֽרְמָחִים “polish the spears.”

(2) to cleanse, by washing, or anointing; compare תַּמְרוּקִים. (Syr. ܡܪܰܩ. to wash off.)

Pual מֹרַק pass. of No. 1, to be scoured, Lev 6:21.

Derivatives, מְרוּקִים, תַּמְרוּקִים.
